title: Non-root User permissions for subdirectories
body:
### Issue Description

I'm trying to move my docker workflow to podman and I'd like to understand how to bind mount local directories as a non-root user. I seem to be missing something and hope this might point me in the right direction. There seems to be a difference with what users owndership docker applies vs what podman does , and I'm not sure how to remedy it.

I have a repository at https://github.com/svanellewee/test-podman to test this, however i'll give the broad strokes here first:

Test command (added as `make run-direct`) :
(podman via `podman-docker` wrapper)

```bash
        docker run -it --rm --name fury  \
                -v ./some_subdir:/home/ripley/some_subdir \
                nostromo bash
```
### Expected behaviour (On docker)

when I run `ls` on the container the `some_subdir` directory is the non-root user (as expected):

```bash
ripley@c3db054b8879:~$ ls -ahtlr
total 28K
-rw-r--r-- 1 ripley ripley  807 Mar 27  2022 .profile
-rw-r--r-- 1 ripley ripley 3.5K Mar 27  2022 .bashrc
-rw-r--r-- 1 ripley ripley  220 Mar 27  2022 .bash_logout
drwxrwxr-x 2 ripley ripley 4.0K May  5 21:21 some_subdir
drwxr-xr-x 1 root   root   4.0K May  5 21:25 ..
drwxr-xr-x 1 ripley ripley 4.0K May  5 21:25 .
ripley@c3db054b8879:~$ 
```

### The problem (On podman)

when I run the same command and check `ls` I see `root` user still owns `some_subdir`:

```bash
ripley@19233692dad5:~$ ls -ahtlr
total 12K
-rw-r--r--. 1 ripley ripley  807 Mar 27  2022 .profile
-rw-r--r--. 1 ripley ripley 3.5K Mar 27  2022 .bashrc
-rw-r--r--. 1 ripley ripley  220 Mar 27  2022 .bash_logout
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root   root     16 May  5 20:31 some_subdir
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root   root     12 May  5 21:28 ..
drwxr-xr-x. 1 ripley ripley   22 May  5 21:28 .
ripley@19233692dad5:~$ 
```

Any help would be appreciated! If this is PEBKAC please also point that out, a fresh perspective might be all I need :-) 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
For a podman container:
1. Set up a virtual machine with linux (*WARNING* I nuke ALL VOLUMES and CONTAINERS as part of my sanity check)
2. install podman-docker and enable `systemctl --user start podman` to make sure the user podman runs.
3. Clone repository https://github.com/svanellewee/test-podman
4. run `make run-direct` to wipe _everything_ create the container 
5. Use the running container to list the ownership on all the files (Expected should be a `non-root` user, but will be root)



### Describe the results you received

The mounted directory `some_subdir` do not belong to the new non-root user:
```bash
ripley@19233692dad5:~$ ls -ahtlr
total 12K
-rw-r--r--. 1 ripley ripley  807 Mar 27  2022 .profile
-rw-r--r--. 1 ripley ripley 3.5K Mar 27  2022 .bashrc
-rw-r--r--. 1 ripley ripley  220 Mar 27  2022 .bash_logout
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root   root     16 May  5 20:31 some_subdir
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root   root     12 May  5 21:28 ..
drwxr-xr-x. 1 ripley ripley   22 May  5 21:28 .
ripley@19233692dad5:~$ 
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expect `some_subdir` to be owned by a non-root user.

```bash
ripley@c3db054b8879:~$ ls -ahtlr
total 28K
-rw-r--r-- 1 ripley ripley  807 Mar 27  2022 .profile
-rw-r--r-- 1 ripley ripley 3.5K Mar 27  2022 .bashrc
-rw-r--r-- 1 ripley ripley  220 Mar 27  2022 .bash_logout
drwxrwxr-x 2 ripley ripley 4.0K May  5 21:21 some_subdir
drwxr-xr-x 1 root   root   4.0K May  5 21:25 ..
drwxr-xr-x 1 ripley ripley 4.0K May  5 21:25 .
ripley@c3db054b8879:~$ 
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I ran podman on a fedora 38 virtual machine and docker on my ubuntu host. My docker version specs are:

```
Client: Docker Engine - Community
```

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: If you run with `--userns=keep-id` does it work the way you expect?

Thanks for the feedback @rhatdan ! So I managed to try it now and even though the ownership does change, I still can't see/change that mounted directory

```
starbuck@50dbc2162495:~$ ls -ahltr
total 12K
-rw-r--r--. 1 starbuck starbuck  807 Mar 27  2022 .profile
-rw-r--r--. 1 starbuck starbuck 3.5K Mar 27  2022 .bashrc
-rw-r--r--. 1 starbuck starbuck  220 Mar 27  2022 .bash_logout
drwxr-xr-x. 1 starbuck starbuck   16 May  5 20:31 some_subdir
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root     root       16 May  6 21:29 ..
drwxr-xr-x. 1 starbuck starbuck   76 May  6 21:29
starbuck@50dbc2162495:~$ ls some_subdir/
ls: cannot open directory 'some_subdir/': Permission denied
starbuck@50dbc2162495:~$ 
```

Used this command to mount (podman via `podman-docker`)

```
run-direct: build
        docker run -it --userns=keep-id --rm --name $(CONT_NAME)  \
                -v ./some_subdir:/home/$(CONT_USER)/some_subdir \
                $(DOCKER_TAG) bash

```

SELinux is blocking access. Your docker daemon must not be running with SELinux.


Try
```
podman run -it --userns=keep-id --rm --name $(CONT_NAME)  \
                -v ./some_subdir:/home/$(CONT_USER)/some_subdir:Z \
                $(DOCKER_TAG) bash
```
That seems to do the trick! Thanks @rhatdan ! Closing.
